stimulus: A student is researching brown dwarfs, substellar objects too small to sustain hydrogen fusion. The rotation period of a brown dwarf is the time it takes the object to spin once on its axis. Looking at the table, the student concludes that ______blank
stem: Which choice most effectively uses data from the table to complete the statement?

answerOptions.content: BD-088 takes longer to complete one rotation than any of the other brown dwarfs in the table.
BD-419 and BD-302 take the same amount of time to complete one rotation.
BD-156 takes the longest amount of time to complete one rotation of any brown dwarf in the table.
BD-217 and BD-088 each take more than three hours to complete one rotation.

rationale: Choice C is the best answer. According to the table, BD-156 has a rotation period of 13.08 hours, which is the longest period of any brown dwarf listed; since the rotation period is the time required to spin once, BD-156 takes the longest to complete one rotation. Choice A is incorrect because BD-088 has the shortest rotation period in the table (1.74 hours), not the longest. Choice B is incorrect because BD-419 (8.46 hours) and BD-302 (5.92 hours) have different rotation periods. Choice D is incorrect because BD-088 has a rotation period of 1.74 hours, which is less than three hours.
